question.text: The floating rate Government of India bond launched in 2020 has its interest rate linked to which benchmark, with a spread of 35 basis points?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The floating rate GoI bond has its interest rate linked to the prevailing NSC rate plus a fixed spread of 35 basis points (0.35%). The rate resets every six months when interest is paid. NRIs are not allowed to invest. **The Trap:** The 182-day T-Bill rate is the benchmark for Floating Rate Bonds (FRBs) issued as G-Secs, not for this retail savings bond. **Cross-Exam Context:** Government savings bond features are tested in X-A; benchmark rate linkages also relevant in IV. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This bond offers inflation protection through rate resets — suitable for conservative clients who want government-backed investment with protection against falling real returns.
